label: false-dup
a: Access tokens (JWTs issued for API authentication) expire after 15 minutes. Short expiry limits the window of exposure if a token is stolen.
b: Refresh tokens rotate on every use. When a client uses a refresh token, the old token is invalidated and a new one is issued. If a refresh token is used twice (old token used after rotation), both the old and new token are immediately revoked — this detects token theft.
reviewer_notes: Both are about JWT lifecycle, but A is access-token expiry (15 min) and B is refresh-token rotation + theft detection. Different aspects — the tricky-case example in the guide.
